differential_diagnoses: Diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) related to SGLT2 inhibitor use
Hypovolemic/Cardiogenic shock with Type A lactic acidosis
Adrenal insufficiency secondary to chronic corticosteroid use
next_steps: Obtain arterial blood gas (ABG) with electrolytes to calculate anion gap and determine serum pH
Measure serum β-hydroxybutyrate levels to confirm ketoacidosis
Measure serum lactate levels serially to monitor for Type A vs Type B lactic acidosis
Check random serum cortisol level to evaluate for adrenal insufficiency
Perform urine culture and repeat urinalysis to investigate for occult infection
Discontinue SGLT2 inhibitor and metformin immediately
Maintain hemodynamic stability with aggressive fluid management or vasopressor support as guided by clinical response
